stemHtml: A Medium-Range Jet Transport aeroplane is planned to depart from a sufficiently long runway and the correct V-speeds have been calculated. The pilot has not flown this variant of the aircraft before, and it has a longer fuselage than the variant they have flown before. As the tail clearance during rotation is reduced on this longer aircraft, the pilot performs their rotation at a slightly slower (but still acceptable) rate than they would normally. What will this change?

options.a: It will increase the take-off run, but the take-off distance will be reduced since the aeroplane will attain a climb speed that is slightly faster.
options.b: It will increase both the take-off run and the take-off distance and potentially reduce the clearance over low obstacles close to the runway.
options.c: It will improve the climb performance of the aeroplane as this procedure is effectively the same as using an increased V2 procedure.
options.d: It will reduce the climb performance of the aeroplane since the speed will be above V<sub>2</sub>, which is the speed tor maximum climb angle.
